story: Raymond van Barneveld defeated Phil Taylor 7-6 in one of the greatest World Championship finals ever played. Barney came back from 3-6 down to claim his first PDC World title in a dramatic sudden-death leg — a match still talked about to this day.
share_text: On this day in 2007: Barneveld beat Taylor 7-6 in one of the greatest darts finals ever played. #DartsDaily

story: John Lowe threw the first ever televised nine-dart finish on 13 October 1984 at the MFI World Matchplay. He won £102,000 for the perfect leg — a record prize at the time that made headlines around the world.
share_text: On this day in 1984: John Lowe threw the first ever televised nine-dart finish and won £102,000. #DartsDaily

story: Sixteen of the world's top darts players broke away from the BDO to form the World Darts Council — later renamed the PDC. The split was controversial at the time but ultimately transformed darts into the professional sport it is today.
share_text: On this day in 1993: 16 players broke away to form what became the PDC. Darts was never the same again. #DartsDaily

story: John Part became the first non-European player to win a World Darts Championship, claiming the BDO title in dramatic fashion. The Canadian's victory put North America on the global darts map for the first time.
share_text: On this day in 1994: John Part became the first non-European World Darts Champion. Canada made history. #DartsDaily

story: Fallon Sherrock made history at the PDC World Championship by becoming the first woman ever to beat a man at the tournament. Her victory over Ted Evetts was watched by millions and sparked a global conversation about women in darts.
share_text: On this day in 2019: Fallon Sherrock became the first woman to beat a man at the PDC World Championship. Historic. #DartsDaily

story: Michael van Gerwen won his first PDC World Championship title, defeating Peter Wright 7-4 in the final at Alexandra Palace. It was the beginning of an era of total dominance that would redefine the sport.
share_text: On this day in 2014: MvG won his first PDC World title. The beginning of an era of total dominance. #DartsDaily

story: Peter Wright won his first PDC World Championship title at the age of 49, defeating Michael Smith 7-3 in the final. Snakebite had been knocking on the door for years — and finally the door came off its hinges.
share_text: On this day in 2020: Peter Wright won his first World title at age 49. Better late than never. #DartsDaily

story: Gerwyn Price became the first Welsh PDC World Champion, defeating Gary Anderson 7-3 in the final at Alexandra Palace. The Iceman dedicated the title to his family and to Wales.
share_text: On this day in 2021: Gerwyn Price became the first Welsh PDC World Champion. Wales on top of the darts world. #DartsDaily

story: Adrian Lewis won the first of his two consecutive PDC World Championship titles, defeating Gary Anderson 7-5 in the final. Jackpot was just 25 years old and looked set to dominate for a generation.
share_text: On this day in 2011: Adrian Lewis won his first PDC World title aged just 25. Jackpot hit the jackpot. #DartsDaily

story: Michael van Gerwen recorded the highest ever televised three-dart average in history — 123.40 — against Adrian Lewis in the Premier League. It was a performance that left commentators and opponents speechless.
share_text: On this day in 2016: MvG averaged 123.40 in the Premier League — the highest ever televised average in history. #DartsDaily

story: Eric Bristow won the first of his five World Championship titles, defeating Bobby George in the final. The Crafty Cockney would go on to dominate the sport throughout the entire decade of the 1980s.
share_text: On this day in 1980: Eric Bristow won the first of his 5 World titles. The decade belonged to him. #DartsDaily

story: John Part claimed his second World Championship title — his first on the PDC circuit — defeating Mark Walsh in the final. Darth Maple had proven himself across two different tours and two different eras.
share_text: On this day in 2003: John Part won his second World title — this time on the PDC circuit. A true all-time great. #DartsDaily

story: Luke Littler, aged just 16, reached the PDC World Championship final on his debut — captivating the nation and attracting millions of new fans to the sport. He lost to Luke Humphries but announced himself as the future of darts.
share_text: On this day in 2024: 16-year-old Luke Littler reached the PDC World final on debut. The future had arrived. #DartsDaily

story: Luke Littler went one better than his debut final appearance, defeating Michael van Gerwen 7-3 to become PDC World Champion at the age of 17 — the youngest world champion in the tournament's history.
share_text: On this day in 2025: Luke Littler won the PDC World Championship aged 17. The youngest champion ever. #DartsDaily

story: Gary Anderson won his first PDC World Championship title, defeating Phil Taylor 7-6 in a dramatic final. The Flying Scotsman had finally scaled the sport's greatest mountain after years of near misses.
share_text: On this day in 2015: Gary Anderson won his first World title, beating Phil Taylor 7-6 in a classic final. #DartsDaily

story: Eric Bristow won his fifth and final World Championship title, cementing his legacy as the dominant player of the 1980s. That same year, he personally sponsored a young Phil Taylor — changing darts history forever.
share_text: On this day in 1986: Eric Bristow won his 5th World title. That same year he sponsored a young Phil Taylor. #DartsDaily

story: Michael van Gerwen won his second PDC World Championship, defeating Gary Anderson 7-3 in a commanding final performance. MvG was in a class of his own and showed no signs of slowing down.
share_text: On this day in 2017: MvG won his second PDC World title with a dominant display. A class apart. #DartsDaily

story: Ted Hankey, known as The Count, won the BDO World Championship in one of the most memorable finals of that era. His dramatic walk-on and theatrical style made him one of the most colourful champions in the tournament's history.
share_text: On this day in 2009: Ted Hankey — The Count — won the BDO World Championship in dramatic style. #DartsDaily

story: Peter Wright won his second PDC World Championship title, defeating Michael Smith 7-5 in another thrilling final at Alexandra Palace. Snakebite proved his 2020 title was no fluke.
share_text: On this day in 2022: Peter Wright won his second PDC World title. Snakebite proved he belonged at the very top. #DartsDaily

story: Adrian Lewis defeated Andy Hamilton in the PDC World Championship final to win his second consecutive world title. Jackpot became only the second player after Phil Taylor to win back-to-back PDC World Championships.
share_text: On this day in 2013: Adrian Lewis won back-to-back PDC World titles. Only Taylor had done it before. #DartsDaily

story: John Part won his third and final World Championship title on the PDC circuit, defeating Kirk Shepherd in the final. Darth Maple had now won titles across three different decades — an extraordinary feat.
share_text: On this day in 2008: John Part won his 3rd World title across 3 different decades. An extraordinary achievement. #DartsDaily

story: Andy Fordham won the BDO World Championship title, defeating Mervyn King in the final. The Viking was at the peak of his powers and his victory was celebrated by darts fans across the country.
share_text: On this day in 2004: Andy Fordham — The Viking — won the BDO World Championship. A popular champion. #DartsDaily

story: John Lowe won his first World Darts Championship title — the first of three he would claim across his career. Old Stoneface was at the beginning of a long and decorated journey to becoming a darts legend.
share_text: On this day in 1979: John Lowe won his first World Darts Championship. Old Stoneface was on his way. #DartsDaily

story: Phil Taylor played his final professional match at the PDC World Championship final, losing to Rob Cross. The Power had announced his retirement and the entire darts world stood to salute one of sport's all-time greats.
share_text: On this day in 2018: Phil Taylor played his last professional match. The greatest career in darts history came to an end. #DartsDaily

story: Phil Taylor won his 16th and final PDC World Championship title, defeating Ted Hankey in the final. It was a record that will likely stand forever — 16 world titles in a single sport.
share_text: On this day in 2010: Phil Taylor won his 16th World title. A record that may never be broken. #DartsDaily

story: Gary Anderson won his second consecutive PDC World Championship title, defeating Gary Warriner in the final. The Flying Scotsman had become back-to-back champion and was playing the best darts of his career.
share_text: On this day in 2016: Gary Anderson won back-to-back PDC World titles. The Flying Scotsman at his very best. #DartsDaily

story: Jonny Clayton won the Premier League Darts title, becoming the first Welsh player to claim that trophy. The Ferret from Pembrey had gone from plasterer to Premier League champion in just a few extraordinary years.
share_text: On this day in 2021: Jonny Clayton became the first Welsh Premier League Darts champion. What a story. #DartsDaily

story: Michael van Gerwen won his third PDC World Championship title, defeating Michael Smith 7-3 in the final. MvG was imperious throughout the tournament and firmly established himself as the greatest player of his generation.
share_text: On this day in 2019: MvG won his 3rd PDC World title. The greatest player of his generation at his very best. #DartsDaily

story: Martin Adams won the first of his three BDO World Championship titles at the age of 50 — becoming one of the oldest first-time world champions in the history of any sport. Wolfie had finally howled at the moon.
share_text: On this day in 2007: Martin Adams won his first World title at age 50. One of sport's greatest underdog stories. #DartsDaily

story: Michael Smith won his first PDC World Championship title, defeating Michael van Gerwen 7-4 in an emotional final. Bully Boy had been to the final before and lost — this time there was no stopping him.
share_text: On this day in 2023: Michael Smith finally won the PDC World title after years of near misses. Bully Boy delivered. #DartsDaily
